questions.question: Why is the Right to Information important for consumers?

options.B: It helps consumers make informed choices

questions.explanation: Consumers can check price, quality, quantity and expiry date before purchasing.

questions.question: Which situation violates consumer rights?
questions.explanation: Selling expired medicines violates the Right to Safety.

questions.question: Which consumer right protects against unfair trade practices?
questions.explanation: Right to be informed protects consumers from misleading information.

questions.question: If a product causes harm due to defect, the consumer can:

options.B: File a complaint in consumer court

questions.explanation: Consumers can seek compensation through consumer courts.

questions.question: Which body hears appeals against District Forum decisions?
questions.explanation: State Commission handles appeals from District Forum.

questions.question: Why should consumers insist on a bill?

options.B: As proof for complaint or warranty

questions.explanation: Bill acts as legal proof for redressal.

questions.question: Which mark ensures purity of gold jewellery?
questions.explanation: Hallmark certifies gold purity.

questions.question: What is the main objective of Consumer Protection Act?
questions.explanation: The Act safeguards consumer rights.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of unfair trade practice?
questions.explanation: False advertising misleads consumers.

questions.question: Why is consumer awareness important?
questions.explanation: Awareness helps consumers exercise their rights.

questions.question: Which right allows consumers to select from different products?
questions.explanation: Consumers have freedom to choose among alternatives.

questions.question: FSSAI certification is related to:
questions.explanation: FSSAI ensures food quality and safety.

questions.question: Which consumer right ensures compensation for loss?
questions.explanation: Consumers can seek redressal for damages.

questions.question: Which is NOT a consumer responsibility?
questions.explanation: Consumers should check product details.

questions.question: Which forum handles highest consumer disputes?
questions.explanation: National Commission is the highest consumer court.

questions.question: Right to Safety mainly applies to:
questions.explanation: Consumers must be protected from dangerous goods.

questions.question: Which of the following is a consumer responsibility?
questions.explanation: Consumers should use goods properly.

questions.question: Which quality mark applies to agricultural goods?
questions.explanation: Agmark certifies agricultural products.

questions.question: If a consumer is not satisfied with State Commission decision, appeal can be made to:
questions.explanation: National Commission handles appeals from State Commission.

questions.question: Which right protects consumers from monopoly?
questions.explanation: Right to choose prevents monopoly practices.

questions.question: Why are standard marks important?
questions.explanation: Standard marks assure consumers of quality.

questions.question: Which situation reflects consumer exploitation?

options.B: Underweight goods sold at full price

questions.explanation: Selling underweight goods is unfair practice.

questions.question: Which right ensures knowledge about consumer laws?
questions.explanation: Consumers should be educated about their rights.

questions.question: Which authority sets standards for industrial goods?
questions.explanation: ISI mark ensures industrial product standards.

questions.question: Effective consumer protection requires:
questions.explanation: Both awareness and legal protection are necessary.
